feedback.markdownBody:
# Review CHANGES REQUESTED for PAN-1069

# Review Synthesis — PAN-1069 — 2026-05-12T16:20:18Z

## Verdict: CHANGES REQUESTED

## Context
- Manifest: `/home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1069/.pan/review/agent-pan-1069-review-00d4bba6/context.json`
- Branch: `feature/pan-1069`
- Workspace: `/home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1069`

## Convoy Status
| Sub-role | Status | Blocking findings |
| --- | --- | --- |
| security | done | 0 |
| correctness | done | 2 |
| performance | done | 0 |
| requirements | done | 0 |

## Blocking Findings

### [correctness] Unresolved conflict markers break `spawnRun` compilation — `src/lib/agents.ts:1781`
The changed specialist-conversation block contains raw merge-conflict markers: `<<<<<<< HEAD`, `=======`, and `>>>>>>> origin/main` around the new conversation-creation comments and catch block. These markers are invalid TypeScript syntax, so typecheck, tests, builds, and dashboard startup fail before runtime.

### [correctness] Unresolved conflict markers break conversation DB compilation — `src/lib/database/conversations-db.ts:220`
The `createConversation()` params array contains raw merge-conflict markers around `opts.titleSource ?? 'default'` and `opts.titleSource`. These markers are invalid TypeScript syntax in a changed database module imported by conversation-related tests and dashboard code.

## Non-blocking Findings

### [requirements] Unrelated changes expand beyond the stated hello-route scope — Issue scope
PAN-1069's acceptance criteria cover only a pure `GET /hello` route and router registration. The diff also changes frontend build behavior, legacy specialist parsing, specialist conversation creation, and conversation title defaults. This expands review and merge blast radius beyond the stated issue, but the requirements reviewer marked it non-blocking because the hello-route requirements themselves are complete.

## Clean Sub-roles
- security: no findings.
- performance: no findings.

## Requirement Coverage
The requirements reviewer found all seven acceptance criteria implemented for `/hello`: HTTP 200, exact body `<html><body>Hello</body></html>`, `text/html` content type, registration in the main Effect HTTP router, a new route file, no auth, no database access, and no side effects.

Source: /home/eltmon/Projects/panopticon-cli/workspaces/feature-pan-1069/.pan/review/agent-pan-1069-review-00d4bba6/synthesis.md

## Required action

Fix every blocking review finding, commit the fixes, then re-request review with:

`pan review request PAN-1069 -m "Fixed review issues"`

## REQUIRED: Fix the failing check, push, and request a new review

1. Read the error output above carefully
2. Fix the code causing the failure
3. Run the failing check locally to verify it passes
4. Commit every change
5. Invoke the /rebase-and-submit skill for PAN-1069 — this is an atomic task. Because verification already ran once (a PR exists), the skill will push your branch and run `pan review request PAN-1069 -m "Fixed test"` for you. NEVER curl `/api/review/...` or any dashboard endpoint — `pan review request` is the only supported re-entry point.

Do NOT stop between steps. Do NOT stop after pushing. Do NOT stop until `pan review request` has completed successfully.
